commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR archives temporal publisher-abuse anomalies in a new Convex table and adds a management dashboard Signals tab with bounded counts, paginated signal rows, severity, linked skill/publisher context, and ratio columns.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a current-main bug report. The changed behavior is source-reviewable and the attached local-browser screenshots show the seeded Signals tab workflow.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated follow-up can add the missing security-moderation spec note without changing runtime behavior.

target.title: Add publisher abuse signal review dashboard
target.body: ## Summary This PR turns temporal install/download anomalies into durable staff-review signals instead of treating single-day P99 temporal hits as ban candidates. The management abuse dashboard now has a Signals tab that exposes those archived anomalies with severity, linked skill/publisher context, and install/download ratios, while the existing warning and ban workflow remains separate. The Signals tab count is backed by a bounded indexed read, so the tab can show a real count before staff open the paginated signal table without adding an unbounded dashboard scan. ## What Changed - Publisher abuse scoring: single-day temporal P99 hits no longer escalate a publisher into the potential-ban path on their own; temporal anomaly evidence is archived for staff review. - Convex backend: adds the `publisherAbuseSignals` table, indexed dashboard/list queries, retention policy coverage, and local/dev seed fixtures for signal review. - Management UI: adds the Signals tab, a severity column, skill and publisher links that open in a new tab, install/download ratio columns, table skeleton rows, and stable tab counts. - Dashboard counting: `publisherAbuse:listReviewDashboard` reads at most 26 signal rows through `publisherAbuseSignals.by_last_seen_at` and reports `25+` when capped. ## Workflow ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Daily publisher abuse scan completes"] --> B["Score publisher risk and temporal anomalies"] B --> C{"Ban candidate?"} C -->|"Yes: existing ban workflow"| D["Warning / deadline / ban flow stays separate"] C -->|"No: temporal anomaly only"| E["Archive durable review signal"] E --> F["Dashboard reads bounded signal count"] F --> G["Signals tab shows count before click"] G --> H["Staff reviews severity, ratios, skill, and publisher"] ``` ## Proof Behavioral proof: - Local seeded management fixture at `http://127.0.0.1:3031/management?view=abuse` as `@local-admin` showed `Signals 2` before the Signals tab was opened, with no tab loading badges stuck on the page. - Opening Signals showed exactly two archived temporal signals, including severity, linked skill/publisher context, and 7d/30d/all-time install/download ratios. - The signal archive remains review-only evidence; existing potential-ban rows still come from the publisher abuse nomination path. Screenshot proof: | Screenshot | Claim Proved | URL / State | Viewport / Crop | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | | The dashboard can show `Signals 2` before staff open the Signals tab, while the other abuse tabs remain populated and not stuck in loading. | `http://127.0.0.1:3031/management?view=abuse`, local seeded fixture, signed in as `@local-admin`, Potential ban tab active. | Desktop `1440px` wide full-page capture, because the header metrics, tabs, table, and footer prove the whole management route state. | | | The Signals tab renders the new severity column, linked skill/publisher cells, ratio columns, and the two seeded archived signal rows. | `http://127.0.0.1:3031/management?view=abuse`, local seeded fixture, signed in as `@local-admin`, Signals tab active. | Desktop `1440px` wide full-page capture, because the changed tab, table header, rows, and count are all visible together. | ## Verification Reviewer-checkable behavior: - `publisherAbuse:listReviewDashboard` returns the bounded signal count used by the tab; in the local seeded fixture this rendered as `Signals 2` before `listSignalsPage` was activated. - `publisherAbuse:listSignalsPage` remains skipped until the Signals tab is active, then pages archived signal rows for staff review. Automated checks: - `bun run test -- convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts src/routes/-management.test.tsx` - `bun run ci:static` - `bun run test -- packages/clawhub-admin/test-artifact/cli.artifact.test.ts` - `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit` - `bun run ci:types-build` - `bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable` against dev deployment `admired-dodo-615` - `bun run ci:packages` Not clean in one aggregate run: - `bun run ci:unit` ran 4228 tests successfully but timed out the packed admin CLI artifact test in the full suite; the exact timed-out test passed immediately when rerun directly with `bun run test -- packages/clawhub-admin/test-artifact/cli.artifact.test.ts`.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
